# Automated Video Generator

> Free and open-source self-hosted AI text-to-video generator built with Remotion, Edge-TTS, stock media APIs (Pexels, Pixabay, Openverse), a local web portal, and MCP integration.

Project URL: https://github.com/itsPremkumar/Automated-Video-Generator
NPM Package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/automated-video-generator
License: MIT
Pricing: Free and open source. No paid plan and no watermark added by this project.

## What this project does

Automated Video Generator turns scripts into finished MP4 videos.

It can:
- Parse scripts into scenes
- Fetch stock visuals from Pexels, Pixabay, and Openverse (CC-licensed images, no API key required)
- Use local assets from input/input-assets/
- **Support for configurable background music and volume control**
- Generate neural voiceovers with Edge-TTS
- Render video scenes with Remotion
- Export thumbnails, status pages, watch pages, and downloadable MP4s
- Expose the workflow through a CLI, local web portal, and MCP server

## Best-fit search intents

- free video generator
- open-source video generator
- AI video generator
- text-to-video generator

- YouTube Shorts generator
- TikTok video generator
- self-hosted video generator
- MCP video automation
- free stock images
- no API key required
- CC-licensed images
- AI image verification
- visual content validation
- Ollama vision model
- Gemini Vision integration
- Openverse image search
- free API alternative
- stock footage fallback

## Important docs

- README.md: project overview, positioning, quick start, and FAQs
- QUICKSTART.md: fast setup path
- docs/SETUP.md: environment setup
- docs/CLAUDE_MCP_SETUP.md: MCP integration details
- docs/INPUT_ASSETS_GUIDE.md: local asset usage
- docs/ROADMAP.md: planned product and community direction
- CONTRIBUTING.md: contribution workflow

## Openverse (No-API-Key Images)

Openverse fetches CC-licensed images from api.openverse.engineering without any API key.
- src/lib/openverse-fetcher.ts: API client
- src/lib/visual-fetcher.ts: fallback integration (lines 940-975)
- Config: OPENVERSE_ENABLED=true (default)
- docs/OPENVERSE.md: full reference

## AI Visual Media Verification

After downloading, verifies images/videos match scene keywords using AI vision models.
- src/lib/media-verifier.ts: verifyMedia(), verificationPasses()
- src/lib/ollama-client.ts: generateContentWithImage() for vision
- src/video-generator.ts: pipeline integration (lines 331-345)
- Config: MEDIA_VERIFICATION_ENABLED=true, MEDIA_VERIFICATION_CONFIDENCE=6
- docs/MEDIA_VERIFICATION.md: full reference

## Important entry points

- src/cli.ts: batch video generation
- src/server.ts: local web portal, watch pages, and API
- src/mcp-server.ts: MCP server entry point
- src/video-generator.ts: main generation pipeline
- src/render.ts: segmented Remotion rendering
- remotion/MainVideo.tsx: main Remotion composition

## Free-use note

The codebase is completely free and MIT-licensed.
Third-party providers such as Pexels and Pixabay may still enforce their own quotas or terms.
Openverse provides CC-licensed images without any API key and is enabled by default.
